findings.statement: Google's Ironwood (TPU v7) custom-silicon superpod scales to 9,216 chips and Anthropic plans to access up to 1 million TPUs.
findings.why: Hyperscaler custom accelerators add non-merchant AI compute supply, eroding marginal merchant-GPU demand.

impact.mechanism: Each unit of in-house silicon displaces one unit of merchant-GPU demand.

evidence.source: Google Cloud Blog (official) - Ironwood TPU (TPU v7)
evidence.excerpt: 9,216 chips in a superpod ... Anthropic plans to access up to 1 million TPUs

findings.statement: AMD non-GAAP gross margin was 55% in Q1 2026.
findings.why: A 55% non-GAAP margin is healthy for the #2 merchant-GPU vendor but well below NVIDIA's, capping unit-economics strength.

impact.mechanism: Margin reflects pricing power and cost structure in the merchant-GPU unit economics.

evidence.source: AMD Investor Relations press release - AMD Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results

findings.statement: AMD Data Center segment revenue was $5.8B in Q1 2026, up 57% YoY on EPYC and the Instinct GPU ramp.
findings.why: AMD Instinct shipments are a growing alternative supply that erodes NVIDIA merchant-GPU share at the margin.

impact.mechanism: Instinct units substitute for merchant NVIDIA GPUs in AI data centers.

evidence.source: AMD Investor Relations press release - AMD Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
evidence.excerpt: Data Center segment revenue was $5.8 billion, up 57% year-over-year ... continued ramp of AMD Instinct GPU shipments

findings.statement: NVIDIA Data Center revenue was a record $75.2B in Q1 FY2027, up 21% sequentially.
findings.why: Data-center demand remains very strong, though the sequential growth slope has eased from prior quarters.

impact.mechanism: DC revenue QoQ slope is the core merchant-GPU demand-momentum read (D2).

evidence.source: NVIDIA Newsroom (Investor Relations) - Q1 FY2027 results
evidence.excerpt: First-quarter Data Center revenue was a record $75.2 billion ... up 21% from the previous quarter

findings.statement: NVIDIA Data Center revenue was $51.2B in Q3 FY2026, up 25% sequentially.
findings.why: Establishes the prior-quarter point of the DC-revenue growth slope at +25% QoQ.

impact.mechanism: DC revenue QoQ slope is the core merchant-GPU demand-momentum read (D2).

evidence.source: NVIDIA Newsroom (Investor Relations) - Q3 FY2026 results
evidence.excerpt: Record Data Center revenue of $51.2 billion, up 25% from Q2

findings.statement: NVIDIA Data Center revenue was $62.3B in Q4 FY2026, up 22% sequentially.
findings.why: Mid point of the slope; +25% -> +22% -> +21% QoQ shows strong but gently decelerating demand momentum.

impact.mechanism: DC revenue QoQ slope is the core merchant-GPU demand-momentum read (D2).

evidence.source: NVIDIA Newsroom (Investor Relations) - Q4 and Fiscal 2026 results
evidence.excerpt: Record quarterly Data Center revenue of $62.3 billion, up 22% from the previous quarter

findings.statement: NVIDIA inventories rose to $25.8B as of Apr 26 2026, up from $21.4B a quarter earlier.
findings.why: A rising whole-chain inventory level signals supply being built ahead of shipment, a classic glut-risk tripwire.

impact.mechanism: Inventory build is the S10 whole-chain inventory index (supply-side).

evidence.source: NVIDIA Corporation Form 10-Q (Q1 FY2027), Notes 7 & 10 / balance sheet

findings.statement: NVIDIA manufacturing, supply and capacity commitments reached $119B as of Apr 26 2026, plus $30B multi-year cloud commitments.
findings.why: A very large real-money supply/capacity commitment is NVIDIA locking in forward supply, expanding supply momentum.

impact.mechanism: Supply/capacity commitments expand the forward supply track (S10).

evidence.source: NVIDIA Corporation Form 10-Q (Q1 FY2027), Notes 7 & 10 / balance sheet
evidence.excerpt: these commitments were $119 billion ... Multi-year cloud service agreement commitments ... were $30 billion

findings.statement: NVIDIA holds roughly 80-86% of AI-accelerator revenue; AMD 5-7%, Intel <1%.
findings.why: An ~80%+ revenue share in AI accelerators is a dominant moat for merchant GPUs.

impact.mechanism: Market share is the moat read for the category.

evidence.source: Silicon Analysts (citing Bloomberg Intelligence, IDC, NVIDIA SEC filings)
evidence.excerpt: NVIDIA holds ~80% of the AI accelerator market by revenue ... AMD estimated 5-7% share

confidence.basis: analyst synthesis, secondary tier (capped at medium)

findings.statement: Intel cancelled Falcon Shores as a commercial GPU and missed its $500M Gaudi 3 sales goal; Jaguar Shores not expected until 2026.
findings.why: Intel withdrawing a merchant AI GPU removes alternative supply, marginally relieving competitive pressure on NVIDIA/AMD.

impact.mechanism: A would-be alternative supplier exiting reduces non-NVIDIA merchant supply (S9).

evidence.excerpt: leverage Falcon Shores as an internal test chip, without bringing it to market ... unable to meet its goal of $500 million in Gaudi 3 sales

confidence.basis: trade media, secondary tier (capped at medium)

findings.statement: NVIDIA held 92% of the discrete GPU market in Q3 2025 per JPR (AMD 7%, Intel 1%).
findings.why: Prior-quarter discrete-GPU share point; NVIDIA dominance with a small AMD uptick.

impact.mechanism: Market share is the moat read for the category.

evidence.excerpt: NVIDIA held 92% market share in Q3 2025 ... AMD rose to 7% ... Intel achieved a 1% market share

confidence.basis: JPR via trade media, secondary tier (capped at medium)

findings.statement: AWS activated Project Rainier with nearly 500,000 Trainium2 chips; Anthropic expected on >1 million Trainium2 by year-end.
findings.why: Large-scale Trainium deployment is alternative supply that erodes marginal merchant-GPU demand.

impact.mechanism: Each unit of in-house silicon displaces one unit of merchant-GPU demand (S9).

evidence.source: AWS / About Amazon (official) - Project Rainier activation
evidence.excerpt: AWS activated Project Rainier with nearly half a million Trainium2 chips; Claude expected to be on more than 1 million Trainium2 chips by year-end

findings.statement: AMD's MI450/Helios ramps in H2 2026 with Meta (up to 6GW) and OpenAI (6GW) deployments beginning.
findings.why: Multi-gigawatt AMD Instinct commitments expand alternative supply against NVIDIA at the high end.

impact.mechanism: Hyperscaler AMD GPU commitments substitute for merchant NVIDIA supply (S9).

evidence.excerpt: Meta 6GW deal ... shipments ... begin in the second half of 2026 ... OpenAI 6GW deal ... deployment starting H2 2026

confidence.basis: trade media, secondary tier (capped at medium)

findings.statement: NVIDIA held 94% of the discrete/AIB GPU market in Q4 2025 per JPR (AMD 5%, Intel 1%).
findings.why: Latest discrete-GPU share point; NVIDIA dominance widened 10pp YoY.

impact.mechanism: Market share is the moat read for the category.

evidence.excerpt: NVIDIA 94% of the Add-In Board (AIB) market; AMD 5% market share; Intel 1%

confidence.basis: JPR via trade media, secondary tier (capped at medium)

findings.statement: H100 on-demand rental had fallen to about $2.50/GPU-hr by May 2026 (B200 about $6.02/GPU-hr).
findings.why: The market-clearing rental price for last-gen GPUs falling is an early sign of softening marginal demand / slack.

impact.mechanism: GPU rental price is the fastest demand-side clearing-price read (D6).

evidence.source: Spheron Network GPU Cloud Pricing Comparison
evidence.excerpt: H100 SXM5 on-demand: Spheron $2.50/hr ... B200 SXM6 on-demand: Spheron $6.02/hr

confidence.basis: rental price tracker, secondary tier (capped at medium)

findings.statement: Intel guided Q2 2026 non-GAAP gross margin to 39.0% (GAAP 37.5%).
findings.why: Intel's ~39% margin is the weak tail of merchant-compute unit economics, dragging the category read slightly.

impact.mechanism: Margin reflects unit-economics health across the merchant-compute set.

confidence.basis: 8-K via trade media, secondary tier (capped at medium)

findings.statement: NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 non-GAAP gross margin was 75.0% (GAAP 74.9%), with hyperscalers ~50% of Data Center revenue.
findings.why: A 75% gross margin reflects exceptional pricing power and is the dominant unit-economics signal for the category.

impact.mechanism: Margin reflects pricing power in merchant-GPU unit economics.

evidence.source: NVIDIA Corp Form 8-K / Q1 FY2027 press release (via StockTitan)
evidence.excerpt: GAAP and non-GAAP gross margins for the first quarter were 74.9% and 75.0%, respectively

confidence.basis: 8-K via trade media, secondary tier (capped at medium)

findings.statement: H100 rental ranged $1.38-$11.06/GPU-hr by June 2026, off historical ~$8 peaks; clouds cut H100/H200/A100 up to 45%.
findings.why: Steep, broad rental-price cuts corroborate softening last-gen GPU demand / rising slack.

impact.mechanism: GPU rental price is the fastest demand-side clearing-price read (D6).

evidence.source: Thunder Compute - AI GPU Rental Market Trends
evidence.excerpt: H100 80GB pricing now runs from $1.38/hr to $11.06/hr ... leaving behind historical peaks near $8/hr ... cut costs ... by up to 45%

confidence.basis: rental market tracker, secondary tier (capped at medium)

bottleneck.rationale: NVIDIA inventory rose to $25.8B and supply/capacity commitments reached $119B (+$30B cloud) - supply is being built aggressively, easing the prior shortage but seeding glut risk.

competitiveStructure.rationale: Alternative supply is expanding fast - AMD MI450/Helios plus AWS Trainium and Google TPU at ~1M-chip scale - even as Intel exits. The competitive/alternative-supply structure is intensifying against the merchant-GPU incumbent.

moat.rationale: NVIDIA holds ~94% of discrete GPUs and ~80-86% of AI-accelerator revenue - a dominant, durable moat.

momentum.rationale: NVIDIA DC revenue is enormous and still growing (+25% -> +22% -> +21% QoQ), but the slope is flattening and last-gen GPU rental prices are collapsing - strong level, deteriorating momentum.

unitEconomics.rationale: NVIDIA's 75% gross margin dominates the read; AMD healthy at 55%, Intel weak at 39%. Category unit economics are strong, led by NVIDIA pricing power.

narrative: Merchant-GPU demand is still powerful but past its peak momentum: NVIDIA Data Center revenue grew +21% sequentially in Q1 FY2027 on a decelerating slope while H100 rental prices collapsed toward ~$1.38-2.50/GPU-hr, signaling softening marginal demand. Supply is ramping hard - NVIDIA committed $119B of capacity and inventory rose to $25.8B - while alternative supply (AMD MI450, AWS Trainium, Google TPU at ~1M-chip scale) intensifies even as Intel exits. NVIDIA's ~80-94% share and 75% gross margin keep its moat and unit economics dominant, but the demand-momentum/supply-build gap is the signal to watch.
